connectorUiConfig.title: Mimecast Events (Push)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Mimecast](https://www.mimecast.com) Events connector ingests security events from the Mimecast Event Push service into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) Push pattern. All Mimecast event types — Secure Email Gateway (Cloud Gateway, DLP), Targeted Threat Protection (URL, Attachment, Impersonation Protect), Audit, Cloud Integrated, and Awareness Training — are routed to the unified `MimecastEvents_CL` table. The `mimecastEventType` column acts as a discriminator so existing KQL queries can be adapted with a simple filter. Auth is Entra client credentials configured on the Mimecast side; no Mimecast API credentials are stored in Microsoft Sentinel.

sampleQueries.description: All Mimecast events (last 24 h) — volume by type
sampleQueries.query:
MimecastEvents_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| summarize count() by mimecastEventType
| order by count_ desc

sampleQueries.description: TTP URL events — blocked or malicious URLs
sampleQueries.query:
MimecastEvents_CL
| where mimecastEventType == 'ttp_url'
| where scanResult == 'malicious' or action == 'Block'
| extend body = todynamic(mimecast_body)
| project TimeGenerated, senderAddress, recipientAddress, url, scanResult, action

MimecastEvents_CL
| where mimecastEventType == 'ttp_attachment'
| where action == 'Block' or scanResult !in ('clean', '')
| project TimeGenerated, senderAddress, recipientAddress, fileName, fileHash, scanResult, action

sampleQueries.description: SEG Cloud Gateway — top senders by event count
sampleQueries.query:
MimecastEvents_CL
| where mimecastEventType == 'seg_cg'
| summarize EventCount = count() by senderAddress
| top 20 by EventCount desc

MimecastEvents_CL
| where mimecastEventType == 'audit'
| extend body = todynamic(mimecast_body)
| summarize count() by auditType
| order by count_ desc

dataTypes.name: MimecastEvents_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
MimecastEvents_CL
| summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnotempty(Time)

value:
MimecastEvents_CL
| summarize LastLogReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| project IsConnected = LastLogReceived > ago(7d)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: Microsoft Entra
customs.description: Permission to create an app registration in Microsoft Entra ID. Requires Entra ID Application Developer role or higher.

customs.name: Microsoft Azure
customs.description: Permission to assign the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role on the Data Collection Rule (DCR). Requires Azure RBAC Owner or User Access Administrator role.

customs.name: Mimecast Event Push
customs.description: An active Mimecast subscription with access to the Event Push (SIEM) integration settings in the Mimecast Administration Console.

instructionSteps.title: 1. Deploy Connector Resources
instructionSteps.description: This connector enables Mimecast to push security events directly to Microsoft Sentinel via the Azure Monitor Logs Ingestion API.

parameters.content:
#### Automated Deployment
Clicking **Deploy** will create:
- The `MimecastEvents_CL` custom log table
- A Data Collection Rule (DCR) with ingestion-time field extraction
- A Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) for log ingestion
- A Microsoft Entra application with OAuth 2.0 client credentials
- The required **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** role assignment on the DCR

This allows Mimecast to securely push events using OAuth 2.0 client credentials targeting `scope = https://monitor.azure.com/.default`.

> **Note:** No Mimecast credentials are stored in Microsoft Sentinel. Authentication is configured entirely on the Mimecast side.

instructionSteps.title: 2. Copy Configuration Values
instructionSteps.description: After deployment completes, copy the following values into the Mimecast Administration Console to configure the Event Push SIEM integration.

parameters.label: Application (Client) ID
parameters.placeholder: Deploy the push connector above to obtain the Application ID

parameters.label: Client Secret
parameters.placeholder: Deploy the push connector above to obtain the Client Secret

parameters.label: Data Collection Endpoint URI
parameters.placeholder: Deploy the push connector above to obtain the DCE endpoint URI

parameters.label: Data Collection Rule Immutable ID
parameters.placeholder: Deploy the push connector above to obtain the DCR immutable ID

instructionSteps.title: 3. Configure Mimecast Event Push
instructionSteps.description: Log in to the Mimecast Administration Console and configure the SIEM / Event Push integration using the values collected in Step 2.

parameters.content:
#### Mimecast Administration Console Setup

1. Sign in to the [Mimecast Administration Console](https://login.mimecast.com/).
2. Navigate to **Administration > Account > Account Settings > Enhanced Logging**.
3. Select the **Microsoft Sentinel** SIEM integration (or create a new integration).
4. Enter the following values:
   - **Token endpoint:** `https://login.microsoftonline.com/{Tenant ID}/oauth2/v2.0/token`
   - **Scope:** `https://monitor.azure.com/.default`
   - **Client ID:** Application (Client) ID from Step 2
   - **Client Secret:** Client Secret from Step 2
   - **Ingestion Endpoint:** Data Collection Endpoint URI from Step 2
   - **DCR Immutable ID:** Data Collection Rule Immutable ID from Step 2
   - **Stream name:** `Custom-MimecastEvents`
5. Select the event types to push (SEG, TTP, Audit, Awareness Training, Cloud Integrated).
6. Save and enable the integration.

> **Event types and routing:** All enabled event types are routed to the unified `MimecastEvents_CL` table. Use `mimecastEventType` to filter by type (e.g. `ttp_url`, `seg_cg`, `audit`). See the sample queries tab for examples.

instructionSteps.title: 4. Verify Data Ingestion
instructionSteps.description: After enabling the integration, allow 5–15 minutes for events to appear. Run the KQL query below to confirm data is flowing.

parameters.content:
```kql
MimecastEvents_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| summarize count() by mimecastEventType
| order by count_ desc
```

If no data appears after 15 minutes:
- Verify credentials in the Mimecast Administration Console.
- Check that the stream name in Mimecast is exactly `Custom-MimecastEvents`.
- Confirm the DCR Immutable ID does not include a leading `dcr-` prefix if your tool strips it.
- Review Azure Monitor diagnostic logs for ingestion errors.
